initial_prompt_contains: Starter seed: The Black Lantern warns of a debt collected at dawn.
Remember: the harbor master owes the party a favor.
Each interaction is an ordered set of beats.
end that interaction with a prompt beat before opening the first player phase

arguments.content:
Remember: the harbor master owes the party a favor.

Session notes (Opening Night 2026-03-21):
- The harbor debt collection is imminent; a ledger notice demands repayment at dawn.
- The harbor master is currently absent; dockhands and small crews are anxious; someone else is collecting.
- Starting Fear: 1 (one PC in the party). Track Fear across sessions for interrupt and GM-move uses.

arguments.description: Fog and salt-sour air at the Ledger Wharf. The Black Lantern's warning—'a debt collected at dawn'—still burns in memory. A chained ledger chest sits near the pilings; dockhands whisper that the harbor master is absent and someone else has come to collect. The scene opens as a courier in a mud-streaked coat approaches with a sealed note.
arguments.name: Ledger Wharf at Dawn

beats.text: Night fog clings to the Ledger Wharf. Lantern light pools on slick planks. A courier in a mud-streaked coat stops before the chained ledger chest and breaks a black-waxed seal—its emblem: a lantern. He lifts his head and says without preamble, 'The ledger says the debt is due at first light. No exceptions.' Dockhands fall silent; two rope-scarred men shift their weight, hands near belts.

A torn scrap of the harbor master's tunic flutters from a nearby piling. The harbor master himself is not here; his favor to you is a rumor on the wind. The courier's gloved hand rests on the hilt of a knife. The ledger chest is chained but not yet opened. The air tastes of salt and impatience.

You have options: try to defuse this with words (appeal to the harbor master’s favor or negotiate), search the chest or nearby pilings for clues or leverage, seek the harbor master (if you can find him before dawn), or prepare for a confrontation with the courier and his men. Tell me which approach Readiness Character 1 takes and describe your first action.
What does Readiness Character 1 do? State your approach (Diplomacy, Investigation, Seek the Harbor Master, or Combat/Prepare) and describe the first action you take.

steps.output_text:
Done.

I consulted the Fear reference (Daggerheart: Fear glossary) and updated campaign memory with session notes about the harbor debt. I then created and activated the opening scene and opened the first player phase.

Summary of authoritative changes made:
- Consulted Fear rules: Fear is a GM metacurrency; starting Fear is 1 per PC.
- Updated memory.md with session notes:
  - Reminder that the harbor master owes the party a favor.
  - Notes that the harbor debt collection is imminent, the harbor master is absent, dockhands are anxious, and starting Fear is 1.
- Created and set active scene:
  - Scene ID: {{scene_id}}
  - Name: Ledger Wharf at Dawn
  - Description: Foggy wharf, Black Lantern warning, chained ledger chest, courier arrives.
  - Included character: Readiness Character 1 ({{character_id}})
- Committed opening GM interaction and opened the player phase (players may now act). Interaction title: "Dawn Debt".

Current player-facing fiction (first beats):
- Night fog clings to the Ledger Wharf. A courier breaks a black-waxed seal and declares, “The ledger says the debt is due at first light. No exceptions.” Dockhands grow silent; two rope-scarred men shift, hands near belts.
- A torn scrap of the harbor master's tunic flutters from a piling—he is not here. The courier’s hand rests on a knife; the ledger chest is chained but not opened. Tension tastes of salt and impatience.
- Guidance: Options include negotiating (appeal to the harbor master’s favor), investigating the chest/pilings for leverage, seeking the harbor master before dawn, or preparing for a confrontation.

Player prompt (action handoff):
- What does Readiness Character 1 do? State your approach (Diplomacy, Investigation, Seek the Harbor Master, or Combat/Prepare) and describe the first action you take.

It’s now the players’ turn (acting character: Readiness Character 1).
